poem.content:
The furnace hummed, a dinosaur's deep breath,
as sunset painted the western sky a blush.
No artwork hung, only shadows underneath
the beam that promised soon I could retire.

The staff had gone, another income segment lost,
another dream destroyed by tidal shifts.
I used to play here, careless, full of dust,
now aging walls whisper desperate gifts.

This crumbling estate, I cannot bring
myself to mend its fractured, fading grace.
I glance outside, at what the future sings -
but fear is etched upon Time's weary face.

Could I predict the ruins I would find?
The ashes where a future used to gleam?
The past extends, a chain around my mind,
until I cannot tell if this is dream.

The cracks like veins crawl where laughter used to bloom.
The furnace flares, and in its dying light,
I see the darkness deepen in the room,
preparing to make everything its night.

October's chill, an olive sky descends,
The host of memories, where youth suspends
Its fragile form. A single file,
Of thoughts, stretching a winding mile.

The range of mountains, stark and gray,
Mirrors the emptiness of yesterday.
My stomach knots, a twisting mess,
For chances lost, I sorely miss.

An eagle circles, vapor trails so thin,
A secret solace, held deep within.
To sustain hope, a fragile, leaking cup,
While destinies converge, rise up.

We watched the merge, a digital tide,
As futures shifted, side by side.
An elephant's grey, in morning light
Reflecting shadows in the night.

A kid's lost giggle, echoes still,
Across the void, against my will.
The heart remembers, what time conceals,
As autumn's tapestry reveals.

A midnight panther, sleek and normal now,
No longer vicious, eyes that held a fight.
The old chief dreamt beside a bridge of bough,
His weathered hand clamped on a fading light.

The budget small, a grant he’d hoped might bloom,
To finish what his fathers had begun.
A genius plan, escaping future gloom,
With nature’s vessel, catching morning sun.

An agent came, with words of modern trade,
A hollow promise, brightly colored, spun.
He saw the ancient wisdom start to fade,
The graceful wheel, no longer meant to run.

The chief refused, a quiet, firm decree,
To let the new world steal what made him free.
His vessel strong, his spirit clear and bright
Amazing strength against approaching night.
He would build his bridge and finish what he started.
The color of hope, now forever imparted.

The solar wind whips, a frozen kiss,
Upon the chrome, a neon race begins.
Youth spills out, a lottery of dreams,
Where fortunes rise and fall in laser beams.

We pave the way with digital despair,
Our lives a clip, suspended in thin air.
Fury simmers, a silent, slow burn,
While monitors flicker, lessons unlearned.

Each dawn a prison, defined by the screen,
Genre-bending boredom, a world unseen.
The ozone cracks, a warning from above,
While we waste our hours on fleeting love.

Long hair streams back, against the digital tide,
A single soul where hope and fury hide.
Can we begin again, escape the waste?
Before our future is forever erased.
Too late, the clip ends, the light is gone.
The race is over, and the future’s pawn.

The cinnamon dust of memory, a taste
of something sweeter than what’s come to pass.
A lottery of glances, heartbeats placed
on risks that shatter like thin hospital glass.

I wear the mean world like a heavy glove,
a shield against the ships that sail the blood
red seas of sorrow.  Notice how they move,
these vessels forged in tides misunderstood.

An oval cage of choices, small and tight,
where price and worth are blurred beyond belief.
To break, to soar, to find the fading light,
requires a desperate, sweet relief.

Popular opinion, a stifling screen,
a filter that distorts what’s truly seen.
I long to shed this level, weary skin,
reveal the raw, defiant soul within.

The ship is gone. The cinnamon's past.
No lottery, no escaping cage.
The mean world waits, a shadow built to last.
The glove held tight on life's hard page.

In a town where streets are paved with moonlight,
A fine mist riseth, veiling all in sight.
The vague echoes of a forgotten past,
Whisper secrets, echoes that forever last.

Short treatises on life's fleeting nature,
Are scribbled on the walls, a morbid canvas.
Prosperity and decay, a fragile balance,
Weigh heavily on the hearts of those who dwell in this place.

Also, in the midst of this whirlwind of life,
A nest of worries, lies securely tied.
Please don't disturb the delicate balance,
For fear of unleashing a calamitous dance.

 Memories, like vivid threads, are woven and spun,
Into the tapestry of a life that's just begun.
But in the throng, a faint cry rises loud,
A laundry list of sorrows, nobody hears proud.

An accident, a misstep, a miscalculation too,
Can spin a life astray, leaving the heart in disarray, it's true.
A portion of sorrow, is the price we pay,
Because we dare to dream, to love, to live each day.

Yet, in this town, there's solace for the soul,
A refuge from the madness that makes us whole.
A place where the fine threads of hope entwine,
A reminder that even in darkness, there's a light divine.

The salt air whispers, "Hello," a digital sea,
Where dreams begin, and wallets flow so free.
A fabric of chance, woven tight and deep,
Where fortunes slumber, secrets softly sleep.

The endless waves crash, a rhythmic, hungry sound,
While hungry folks all sit around.
For a tuna burger with special sauce,
Hoping today will have no loss.

The faculty stands, on shores of grit and strife,
Preparing souls for this unusual life.
To educate the young, to shape the minds so bright,
And guide them through the day and darkest night.

A dove descends, as transactions now approve,
New contracts blossom, fueled by endless groove.
Some find it able, to pay the climbing tuition,
While others struggle.

So let us find our grit and thrive,
And from our digital world, we shall survive.

A nerve frayed, a hope worn short,
a key lost in the echoing vault.
Muscles tense, unable to sustain,
the weight of promises, a crystal rain.

A brush with fate, a mimic's art,
concealing wounds carved in the heart.
A token given, an empty prize,
guarded by ghosts with hollow eyes.

Is this transaction valid, this whispered plea?
An endorsement sought, eternally.
We miss the signals, the subtle signs,
adrift in debris, where sunlight declines.

The ghost of a choice, a shadow play,
where strength is fleeting, slipping away.
But somewhere within, a flicker persists,
a fragile bloom amidst hardened fists.

A whispered prayer, a silent call,
hoping something breaks, before we fall.
We brush off the dust, and face the unknown,
a single seed, desperately sown.

The air turned brisk, a forest's breath,
as a couple strolled, near insane with grief.
He winked, a gesture shadowed by the trees,
she tilted her head, beyond relief.

A vendor stood, by a road ahead,
his cart piled high, her grocery need.
She thought about escape, about a plunge
into depths where silent sorrows bleed.

But sunshine, warm, upon her face,
a jewel-like promise, caught in a blur.
A moment held, in fragile lace,
renew the hope, the insane thoughts deter.

He took her hand, a silent vow,
to stand with her, here and now.
The forest whispers, soft and deep,
while through the trees their secrets creep.
The grocery bag, a weight they bore,
they walk ahead, and nothing more.

I lend my ear to fiction, spun of night,
where stars are false and shadows bleed like fire.
This rifle script, a future caught in flight,
a family choked, fulfilling dark desire.

I learn to swim against the churning tide,
to clarify the murk that hides the truth.
A year has passed, and still the pain won't hide,
a constant echo from my wasted youth.

A kind hand reaches, I can not receive,
the darkness clings, possessive and unkind.
I watch the ocean, and I start to grieve,
for all the things I left so far behind.

The target shifts, a phantom in the haze,
a fishing boat upon the distant shore.
A false promise in these sunlit days,
I learn to swim, and I can only implore,
the fire inside will simply cease to be,
and clarify a path for only me.

A voyage on vapor, a canvas gray,
The ocean breathes a salty spray.
A mystery unfolds in watery dance,
While shadows shift, a fleeting glance.

A payment due, for passage deep,
Where ancient secrets softly sleep.
An apple rots, a bitter juice,
A creeping dread, insidious.

Disease whispers on the breeze,
Rustling pages of the book of trees.
He wears a sock, now frayed and thin,
A drip, drip, drip, where do I begin?

To understand the tangled thread,
Around her neck, by sorrow led.
The next port calls, a distant shore,
Where scattered thoughts will be no more.

To find a solace in the air,
And watch the world without a care.
The dance continues, life’s refrain,
Amidst the vapor and the rain.

The desert stretches, where useless dreams
Acquire a phantom weight, like beefy limbs
Of ancient cactus, tasting ash and spite.

A general's ghost wanders in the light,
Also seeking what he could not bring--
A gentler fate, before the vultures sing.

The battlefield, a split in time's own face,
Reveals the rose, in this forsaken space.
No silk to ease the pain, no solace found,
Just lion's roar upon the barren ground.

The taste of loss, a bitter draught to hold,
A story whispered, never to grow old.
He sought to bring a solace, pure and deep,
But found only silence, where secrets sleep.

If I could split the sky, and show you then,
The useless burden carried by all men,
The cactus blooms, in space so very wide,
A desert bloom, where silent wishes hide.

In the shadow of the stone, where evil whispers low
Lurks a world behind the scenes, where few may go
The frequent footsteps echo, a sinister refrain
As darkness creeps, a kangaroo's shadow cast in vain

Its fur as white as alabaster, its eyes as black as night
It hops with menace, a symbol of a twisted delight
A public spectacle, a show of wicked glee
A reminder that behind the stone, evil is free to be

The drip of a faucet, a steady heartbeat in the dark
A rhythm that entices, a loop of cruel charm to embark
A dress of deceit, a cloak of darkness worn
A disguise to keep the innocent from harm, from being torn

But I am an inmate, a prisoner of my own design
Trapped behind the panel, with no escape in sight, no vine
To climb and rise above, to break the chains that bind
A willing captive, a servant to the evil I've left behind

I try to keep my heart from breaking, from shattering like glass
But the wave of despair crashes, and I am forever aghast
At the reflection staring back, a stranger in a familiar face
A mask that resembles mine, but with a wicked, evil pace

I'm lost in a world of shadows, where the light is hard to find
Where the stone is cold and unforgiving, and the kangaroo's eyes are blind
But still I'm ready to face it, to walk the darkened path
For in the end, it's not the evil that consumes me, but the aftermath.

The morning broke, a summer kissed with dew,
But woke me feeling sick, a slight unease.
An antique clock chimed, its mournful tune,
An anchor chained to memories, if you please.

The social hum outside, a distant drone,
While I, the actor, rehearse a cheerful face.
I’ll pave a smile on, though spirit’s never known
Such heavy clouds, such desolate space.

A critic whispers doubt, a chilling breeze,
That I am but a sham, a gilded lie,
And even silk against my skin, if you please,
Can't mask the truth reflected by my eye.

I chuckle weakly, knowing it’s no use
To wallow long in self-deprecating art,
So I embark on this day's twisted truce,
Despite the bitter taste within my hurt.
Never again will I let it take hold of me.
I'll let it pass, let it go to the sea.

The early sun, an oval stain,
behind the fading indigo.
A bachelor dawn, recalling rain,
upon the oyster's echo.

No tourist yet disturbs the shore,
where grains of sand like dreams reside.
An infant wave, then nothing more,
as carbon ghosts begin to hide.

We work, a project yet untold,
to dish out futures, brave and bright.
A secret story to unfold,
like foil reflecting back the light.

From output small, ideas grow,
We must develop what is sown -
though some may see just empty show,
a wasteland they have always known.

The world behind is yesterday,
We dream a new dawn every day.
New worlds will rise, we work to show,
these rising worlds will start to grow.

The winter harbor, a steel grey peace,
a thing forgotten, a peasant's lease.
My face, reflected in the icy pane,
a habit formed in sun and stinging rain.

I saw the van pull in, no license bright,
delivering shadows in the fading light.
A guitar slept within its case of hide,
a prize unclaimed, where truths confide.

No warm embrace within this frozen scene,
but evidence of what has always been.
A fractured domain, where dreams take root,
and bitter winds bear sorrow's somber fruit.

We stand together, ghosts in frosted air,
watching futures bloom from cold despair.
To develop hope where none could seem to be,
our only solace on this frozen sea.

Perhaps the guitar will sing a warmer song,
and banish shadows where they don't belong.
Perhaps the sun will melt this icy heart,
and tear the winter domain apart.

A proof of daring, sketched in virtual light,
Where mansions shimmer, built on whispered code.
A wise retreat, when shadows start to bite,
To build a nest where weary souls unload.

Assume no safety, though the walls are high,
For vintage errors linger in the core.
A kidney aches, beneath a digital sky,
A long and silent scream, unheard before.

Embrace the glitch, the differ-ence it weaves,
A tapestry of fortune, fraught with pain.
A whispered tip, the algorithm believes,
Can soothe the burning, ease the pouring rain.

But pills won’t cure the soul's long, hollow plea,
Nor fix the fragments scattered on the breeze.
A virtual promise, eternally
adrift, among the digital, haunted trees.

The smoke of a broken pact lingers tonight,
A trial by fire, a dim and fading light.
Junior dreams dissolving in the haze,
Lost in the carbon dark of bygone days.

A gentle solace, a memory of silk,
A brush against my cheek, sweet as fresh milk.
But now the menu offers only grief,
And hope's frail amount brings no relief.

I need an anchor in this restless sea,
A bridge to cross, to finally be free.
To deliver myself from this heavy chain,
To repair the damage, ease the pain.

The waves crash hard, a constant, mournful drone,
I search for kindness, for a whispered tone.
A way to mend what’s shattered on the floor,
And find my footing, searching for the shore.
Though silk is torn, the pact betrayed in night,
I seek repair, and walk on, holding light.

The north wind whispers, a lesson harsh,
to a pupil lost, just past life's arch.
He fled the party, the glittering lens,
for a route less traveled, no gentle defense.

Above him, the stars, a simple guide,
a benefit sought, where truths reside.
He yearned for a melody, clear and bright,
to banish the shadows, and bring him to light.

The school of hard knocks, no easy test,
to quit now would mean he’d never truly rest.
The world, a harsh master, lessons unseen,
through a different lens, everything serene.

Though shadows dance and darkness grips,
he walks on, the truth escapes his lips.
A benefit found in quiet space,
a simple truth, a gentle grace.

The route ahead, still dimly lit,
but he will not quit, not even a bit.
He stands again, above the fray.
And finds a new lens, another way.

The early mist, a candy-sweet despair,
hung heavy, like a smoke-filled, distant prayer.
My nerve, a frayed and brittle, broken thing,
awaiting some cool quarter that will ring.

A deer I saw, its eyes like liquid coal,
a critic’s gaze, dissecting my lost soul.
I mixed my hope with shadows on the ground,
where shattered dreams and satoshi are found.

A dose of courage, bitter, hard to take,
beneath the brim of a forgotten hat I make
a stand against the night, the rising tide
of dark demand where fragile hopes subside.

This endless series bends, a tangled chain,
binding me to sorrow and to pain.
The world keeps spinning, deaf to silent pleas,
a concrete jungle, swallowing whole with ease.
No savior comes, no hand to lead me through;
just ash and ember, sky of endless blue.

The hospital clock, a slow indictate of dread,
Each hour a hammer blow upon my head.
A myth spun of healing, a white walled lie,
While shadows gathered, and hope began to die.

A loud insistent call, a surgeon's drill,
To sample frail flesh, against my will.
Muscles bound so tight, a painful bind,
Leaving all peace and solace far behind.

Later, escaping south, a cabin small,
Where I recall the sight, the ghostly thrall.
The wind will reopen wounds, once neatly sealed,
A pain now known, a truth at last revealed.

I try to drill deep, find strength to mend,
Before this anguish brings about my end.
The muscles ache, a ghostly, haunted shore,
Where healing waits, or death will evermore,

Bind my soul, to shadows left behind,
And indicate there's not much to find.

A digital fog, a frozen aspect gleaned,
The general feeling of a world unseen.
Prepare the lab, for echoes faint and low,
Where icy lizard dreams begin to flow.

Retreat into cushions, softly deep,
While frosted windows secrets keep.
Again, the shadow lengthens on the wall,
As digital material starts to fall.

Oppose the chill, the creeping, icy dread,
Decorate with warmth, though hope feels dead.
Afford the fire, let the embers gleam,
A fragile island in a frozen dream.

The aspect shifts, the fog begins to lift,
A lizard’s eye reveals an offered gift.
And though the general mood is stark and wan,
Prepare, retreat, let warmth be born again.

Sun bleeds through the library pane,
dust motes dancing in a golden rain.
Regret, a vacuum in my chest,
a toy horse broken, put to rest.

The stairs ascend, a daunting height,
a prize awaits, bathed in pale light,
but I gather only shadows there,
a few bright hopes, thin as air.

The entire world feels out of sync,
a lesson learned, a bitter drink.
The impact of choices, sharp and deep,
a secret promise I couldn't keep.

A gap between what is and seems,
a trick of fate, disrupting dreams.
The horse now gone, its silent plea,
leaves only emptiness mocking me.

The early rose, a blush against the grey,
Heard the ocean talk, learned what the tides convey.
About the law, the shifting, hungry sand,
And how the fury claws across the land.

A company of shadows, drawing near,
Behind each mask, a chilling, silent fear.
We depend on whispers, carried on the breeze,
Among the lemon groves and bending trees.

To laugh is to invite a cruel test,
To show the happy heart within your breast.
But smiles are fragile, easily undone,
This world can damage all beneath the sun.

He pulled the mask, revealing what remained,
A face the fury of the ocean stained.
No rose survives the bitterness of brine,
No lemon brightens what the tides define.

He learned the law: existence is the fight,
Against the crushing waves and endless night.
And yet, the tide will turn, they say,
Washing the damage into yesterday.

The old vendor, still as stone,
kept a record in his bone.
Of thunder, a Latin curse,
echoing through the universe.

He watched the oven slowly rise,
a helmet of heat across the skies.
The term was up, the debt unpaid,
his weary soul, a game betrayed.

They came to destroy all he possessed,
he saw their shadows, darkly dressed.
A metal frame within the door,
squeezing tight against the floor.

He wanted to quit, to run away,
but concrete held him fast that day.
He raised a hand, a futile plea,
his skin, a map of misery.

A broken leg, a silent scream,
his shattered vision, a fever dream.
The still, small voice within his head,
whispered of ashes, softly spread.

A little silver scale, dusted with galaxy light,
measured the wonder, the night.
We came to collect, luggage in hand,
a hidden butter dish in this promised land.

The action unfolds, a slow, deliberate glide,
like saplings leaning, where secrets reside.
A lone tree stands, witness to all,
as shadows lengthen and softly call.

The setup complete, cement barely dry,
underneath a painted azure sky.
The amount of longing, a heavy weight,
a kitchen forgotten, sealed by fate.

The cosmic dust, it begins to gleam,
an impossible, unfinished dream.
Too much to bear, this burden unknown,
seeds of sadness we've sown.

The scale tips now, towards shadowed grey,
as the universe continues into the next day.
A little is gained, much is lost, it seems,
in the churning galaxy, and half-remembered, fading dreams.

The water, slow and deep, a swamp's dark treat,
Where secrets sleep beneath the cypress knees.
A dwarf's lost name, a forgotten street,
Whispered on the fringe of memories.

The insect hum, a possible doom,
A huntsman's echo in the fading light.
So slow the days, they stumble through the gloom,
And dreams now drop like feathers in the night.

He hunts for answers in the humid air,
A dumb pursuit, a foolish, fruitless quest.
For other worlds exist beyond compare,
Where easy sleep offers a peaceful rest.

But something stirs, a truth he cannot name,
A feeling crawls, like spiders on his skin.
He’s playing a fool, caught in a cruel game.
The swamp is only where the strange events begin!

He searches now with hope, that he can reap
A treasure of wisdom, from the waters deep.

The sun, a cracked photo, illegal in its glare,
baked the frost to jelly on the window pane.
A cricket, chirping insane rhythms, drew near,
a siren's call across the dusty lane.

I shouldn't rely, but I do, a weakness, old,
like a shoulder worn from years of suffer.
He promised to renew, a story often told,
while polishing a pistol, smooth as butter.

He’s on the wrong side. This I know.
The air hangs thick, a pregnant, stifled breath.
A bomb, a promise, waiting down below,
a silent scream, a whisper close to death.

Some things are best left buried in the clay,
beneath the frost, where promises decay.
He suffers, too, I see it in his face,
mirrored, in a haunting, hollow space.

It’s all illegal. And so are we.
Yet here we stand, where we are meant to be.

The coach rattled, dust obscuring sight,
Another town, another name unsaid.
I longed for home, a beacon in the night,
A pear tree blooming where my dreams were bred.

My civil spirit strained, a fragile thread,
Against the rival lurking in my soul.
He sought to damage all I held instead,
To take my estate, make me lose control.

The trend was clear, decline was on the way.
Sustain yourself, the whisper urged me on.
Could I arrive victorious, come what may?
The various trials, battles to be won.

Life’s puzzle hard, a riddle to solve,
Not ever easy, this much I now know.
The pear tree waits, its branches to absolve,
And offer solace when the wild winds blow.

Though paths are obscure and filled with hidden pain,
My home will always call out my name.

The subway rumbles, a bleak heart of iron,
Another region swallowed by the grey.
A pilot’s stare, unblinking, thereon,
Across the tracks, where shadows sway.

He grips his pen, to sketch a life untold,
A loyal dog asleep upon my stool.
Another armed figure, hard and cold,
Within this urban, broken rule.

The lumber hauled, a hood obscures his face,
A buyer waits, with empty, hollow eyes.
No outdoor light to grace this dismal place,
Just fleeting dreams behind a false disguise.

He dreams of ranch, of open, sunlit space,
A sculpted model, posed in morning dew.
But reality brings only this disgrace,
A canvas stained with shades of somber hue.

He tries to bring the loyal feeling back,
Lost in the urban sprawl, and endless chase,
A soul adrift, upon a barren track,
Leaving only the bleak feel of this place.
